findings.summary: optimizer.py wraps infrastructure.core.logging_utils in a try/except ImportError guard that is never needed
evidence: optimizer.py:47-53: try/except ImportError guard around get_logger with LOGGING_AVAILABLE flag; every log call then guarded by if LOGGING_AVAILABLE and logger
findings.suggestion: Remove the try/except guard and LOGGING_AVAILABLE flag; import get_logger unconditionally.

findings.summary: dashboard_generator.py guards matplotlib but defers plotly import inside a function with no module-level sentinel
evidence: dashboard_generator.py:963: 'import plotly.graph_objects as go' deferred inside function body with no PLOTLY_AVAILABLE guard
findings.suggestion: Add try/except ImportError for plotly at module level with a PLOTLY_AVAILABLE sentinel matching the matplotlib pattern.

findings.summary: Files use typing.List/Dict/Optional despite from __future__ import annotations everywhere making them unnecessary
evidence: environment.py line 43: 'from typing import List, Tuple' while from __future__ import annotations is on line 36
findings.suggestion: Replace List[x], Dict[k,v], Optional[x] with builtin equivalents (list, dict, x | None) across infrastructure files.

findings.summary: steganography/core.py uses deprecated tempfile.mktemp() which has a race condition
evidence: Line 141: tmp = Path(tempfile.mktemp(suffix='.pdf', prefix='steg_')) — mktemp is deprecated
findings.suggestion: Replace with: fd, tmp_str = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix='.pdf', prefix='steg_'); os.close(fd); tmp = Path(tmp_str)

findings.summary: run_infrastructure_tests appends --tb=short twice to the pytest command
optimizer.py LOGGING_AVAILABLE boolean guard pattern is not used anywhere else in the codebase
evidence: Lines 47-53 and 269,278,285,299,304: conditional logging pattern unique to this file; all other modules use unconditional get_logger
findings.suggestion: Remove LOGGING_AVAILABLE guard. Use standard logger = get_logger(__name__) at module level.

findings.summary: Codebase inconsistently passes logger as argument vs using module-level logger creating two competing patterns
evidence: log_success, log_header accept Optional[logging.Logger] and callers pass logger explicitly; but pipeline.py uses module-level logger directly
findings.suggestion: Choose one convention: remove Optional[Logger] parameter from log_* helpers or have all callers inject logger.
